description: Capabilities document contains an unknown top-level field. The CapabilitiesDocument schema is OPEN (additionalProperties: true at top level — see acdp-capabilities.schema.json line 105 and RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3) so future versions can advertise new capability flags without a schema bump. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown top-level fields. The `limits` sub-object remains CLOSED (additionalProperties: false at line 102); this fixture exercises top-level openness specifically.

expected.consumer_action: Pass the schema check, route the unknown fields into a catch-all extension map per the implementer-note recipes in RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3 (Rust serde flatten, Python pydantic extra=allow, Go map[string]any, etc.), and continue with the operation that required fetching capabilities.

negative_examples.closed_decoder_failure: An implementation using #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] (Rust), pydantic with extra='forbid' (Python), or zod's .strict() (TypeScript) will fail this fixture. That is a conformance failure: forward-compat tolerance for unknown top-level fields is normative.
negative_examples.limits_extra_field: If the unknown field were placed inside `limits` instead of at the top level, the document MUST be rejected — `limits` is closed (additionalProperties: false). See caps-005 for the fixed-value case and the implementer note in RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.

expected.rationale: The whole point of the open top-level schema is to make it possible to ship new capability flags (push subscriptions, receipts, walks, etc.) without breaking every deployed v0.0.1 consumer the moment the first registry advertises them. A consumer that treats unknown top-level fields as errors will silently fail the next time ACDP grows.
